_note: Neutral MCP server registry. Define each server ONCE here, then project it into a harness's native config with _core/scripts/gen-harness-config.py. Keeps the repo harness-agnostic. Secrets live in .env and are referenced by ${ENV:KEY}; never inline a secret here.

servers.name: filesystem
servers.description: Example (disabled): expose the workflows folder to the model over MCP.

servers.name: crm-example
servers.description: Example (disabled): pattern for a credentialed line-of-business connector (CRM, ERP, ticketing). The secret comes from .env, not from this file.

servers.name: imap-mailbox-example
servers.description: Example (disabled): READ-ONLY access to one IMAP mailbox via a local MCP server script. Scope the entry name to the mailbox (imap-<account>); to add another mailbox, copy this entry under a new name pointing at the same server script with that mailbox's own config env var. Machine paths and credentials never live in this committed file — they come from env vars: IMAP_MCP_SERVER (path to the server script), IMAP_CONFIG (path to that mailbox's credential JSON).
